item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty great. I like the way the containment procedures is written actually makes it an interesting hook with a decent length to it, usually the Special Containment Procedures part of the SCP ends up being the weakest part in an article for me but here it's super strong. The addenda are a little bit shaky in the sentence to sentence writing. It's hard to make realistic interview dialogue for a random bystander and especially hard to write a character who is simultaneously doing a period dialect, the "refers to self as we" trope, and in this case is made of bricks. I can't blame the author for doing their best and coming up shorter than the monumental task of writing something that complex in the span of a log. But I do like the way the interview with -2 reveals the two part SCP is actually a three part SCP and characterizes the bride a bit. Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so underrated. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i love the little troll under the bridge. this is the only reason i'm upvoting. (no im kidding its a cool article)

(note: just good old capitalism at work)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this SCP. Its fairly well written and the topic is interesting but I feel that SCP-4102-2 and the other portions of the article are a bit disconnected, as I don't quite see the link between 4102-2 and the Brooklyn Bridge. Still a good read, +1.

item.title: Re: Shaggydredlocks
item.content:encoded:
Hello! My sincerest thanks for your critique! I greatly appreciate you not pulling back any punches, as it lets me focus on how to better improve any future work I do!

I suppose what I meant to make more obvious was what you're noting, in regards to -1 disappearing. I meant to say something along the lines of "it goes to great lengths to break line of sight and once it does it's gone".

Still, though, thank you for giving my work a look! I'll definitely keep this all in mind later on!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "And if you believe that I have a bridge to sell you!" I like it, especially the implication that the lack of deals may cause the bridge to collapse. Would Foundation be willing to let a few go through to avoid a potential containment breach…hmm

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-4102's anomalous nature did not manifest until 18██
This coincides with the arrest of notorious conman George C. Parker

It makes not a lick of sense to blackbox a date but then, as an aside, tell readers when a thing happen s anyways via high-profile incident anyone can look up in a manner of seconds. When info-sec falls apart this bad, verisimilitude crumbles, and I'm unable to remain invested in the story.

Having finished, things definitely take a turn for the interesting with -2, but unfortunately, there's far too much cruft here that makes this more of a grind than it needs to be. -1 is given an inordinate amount of detail and abilities for what it does, most of this can be done away with and pared down.

Why does it need to have super speed and manuverability when they can't document it disappearing? Can't it simply break line-of-sight and vanish? Why does it need to have magic powers of compulsion and attraction when it's already empathic, persuasive, and targeting vulnerable people?

Aside from that, the first interview acheives nothing. The story grinds to a halt so readers can be told, again, what -1 is capable of.

This has definitely got potential, but it needs work.

item.title: This really hits home for me
item.content:encoded: Being a Native New Yorker myself (I was born in Brooklyn, but my parents - my mom was from Greece & my dad was from Muncie, Indiana, but were living in Staten Island at the time - took me with them back to Staten Island as soon as all of my post-birth stuff was done), this SCP takes on a whole new level of creepy for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very enjoyable article in my opinion. There is an interesting air of mystery about the whole thing, from the shapeshifting conman to the depressed missing persons collective. All in all, a very well done first SCP.

item.title: Re: Hi!
item.content:encoded: Thanks! Nice to be here! :) Looking forward to more fun writing!

item.title: Re: MaliceAforethought
item.content:encoded: Thank you very much! I really appreciate it! :)

item.title: Re: Hi!
item.content:encoded: Welcome aboard, softwatch!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As a first SCP, this is superb! It does an excellent job of being surreal-feeling yet grounded in reality, and the whole article has a delightful weirdness to it that's difficult to pin down. Definite upvote, very well done :)

item.title: Re: Pastarasta1
item.content:encoded: Apologies! Thought the tag was meant more as a general tag for Series V.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i removed "4000" tag since that tag is only for entries of the 4000 contest and this isn't an entry for it.

item.title: Re: Rush
item.content:encoded:
Thank you so much for the kind words!

I just noticed the paragraph you're talking about, and I think that's a typo on my part. Should be fixed now. Thanks for the heads up!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You struck gold in my opinion.

I noticed grammar issues though, specifically. You switch reference from -1 to -2 mid paragraph. Fifth or so paragraph in the Description. That caused me some confusion. But I was able get past it.

Great work here!

item.title: Re: Hi!
item.content:encoded: Thanks a bunch, I really appreciate it!

item.title: Re: Hi!
item.content:encoded: The images are now localized! They just need to be uploaded directly to the page. I also went and resized them so they won't take as long to load.

item.title: Re: Hi!
item.content:encoded:
Hello Zyn,

Yes, I'd appreciate it greatly. Perhaps I didn't code it properly in the SCP description- I'm not really sure to be honest! First time doing one of these. :)

item.title: Re: Hi!
item.content:encoded: Author, your images are currently linked offsite rather than localized. Would you like some help localizing them?

item.title: Re: Taffeta
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much! I'm glad you liked it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is lovely, mildly disconcerting weird with a good sense of atmosphere and when to play its hand.

item.title: Hi!
item.content:encoded:
Hey everyone! I'm softwatch, longtime fan of the site, and this is my first SCP!
I took a story that I heard about a conman trying to sell the Brooklyn Bridge and did my best to spin it into something with more substance and mystery!

Let me know what you think!

Image Credit
—
SCP 4102
SCP 4102 Vaults
